title: Debug The Logic

content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the logic to print **true** if a number is even, else **false**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'true' if N is even, otherwise print 'false'. Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 1 is odd → false, 2 is even → true, 3 is odd → false, 4 is even → true, 5 is odd → false.
All three numbers (10, 20, 30) are even, so each yields true.
1 is odd → false, 2 is even → true.

solution.approach: The parity of an integer can be determined by checking the least significant bit. If N % 2 == 0 (or (N & 1) == 0) the number is even, otherwise it is odd. For each test case we read N, evaluate the condition and output the corresponding string. This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(1)
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print(2)
    # Add generation logic for other cases as needed.
